title: Anomaly-censored training to suppress SC/VSC and pit-lane distortion
summary: The same state-space F1 paper explicitly removes pit in/out laps and safety-car or VSC laps because they distort degradation signals.
rationale: The same state-space F1 paper explicitly removes pit in/out laps and safety-car or VSC laps because they distort degradation signals. Your dataset does not expose those flags directly, so the model may be learning spurious stop correlations from extreme lap-time shocks that are not genuine wear signals.
implementation_hint: Create inferred anomaly flags using race-lap robust z-scores of `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, fieldwide dispersion, and abrupt position changes. Then test two variants: down-weight anomalous rows in training, or train a clean specialist on non-anomalous rows and blend with the full-data model.
expected_effect: Cleaner signal for true pit intent, especially late-race and wet/chaotic segments where raw lap-time deltas are misleading.
risk: Over-filtering can remove precisely the decisive laps before stops; anomaly detection errors may hurt if races are naturally heterogeneous.
